coredata.dc:title: Colorimetry/fluorescence dual-mode detection of gentamicin based on curcumin nanoparticles and manganese dioxide nanosheets
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2025 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Jia, Mu
Xiao, Jingyi
Liu, Menglong
Ren, Yi
Liu, Yiyao
Gao, Xue
coredata.dc:description: This study used curcumin nanoparticles (CNPs) in phosphate buffer solution (pH 9) [CNPs (pH 9)] as a new oxidative substrate to detect gentamicin (GEN). Manganese dioxide nanosheets (MnO2-BSA NS) with excellent oxidizing properties were applied to catalytic oxidize CNPs (pH 9), which resulted in visual color changed from red to orange-yellow under sunlight. Moreover, the fluorescence of CNPs (pH 9) changed from orange to yellow. And we introduced carbon dots (CDs) as a reference signal, the fluorescence of CNPs quenched the fluorescence of CDs to generate a ratiometric fluorescence response. Therefore, we semi-qualitatively determined GEN with the naked eye and quantitatively evaluated by ratiometric colorimetry (I510 nm/I436 nm) and ratiometric fluorescence (F560 nm/F436 nm). The dual-mode immunoassay linearly correlated with GEN ranged from 10−4 μg/mL to 100 μg/mL with a detection limit (LOD) of 4.44 × 10−5 μg/mL and 3.16 × 10−5 μg/mL. Meanwhile, a smartphone-assisted system was designed for sensing of GEN by the distinct color change, providing a dependable, convenient and on-site assay of detecting GEN.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Gentamicin
Curcumin nanoparticles
Dual-mode immunoassay

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Characterizations of MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2
Optical characterization of CNPs, CNPs (pH 9) and CDs
The mechanism of dual-mode immunoassay for GEN
Oxidase activity study of MnO2-BSA NS

xocs:cp-notices.xocs:cp-notice: © 2025 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) TEM image of MnO2-BSA NS; (B) diameter image of MnO2-BSA NS; (C) UV–vis absorption spectra of MnO2-BSA NS; (D) FT-IR spectrum of MnO2-BSA NS.
(A) XPS full spectrum of MnO2-BSA NS; high-resolution XPS spectrum of (B) Mn 2p; (C) O 1s; (D) C 1s; (E) Zeta potential of MnO2-BSA NS, Ab2 and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2.

(A) UV–vis absorption spectra at different pH, insert: the color images of sunlight of CNPs; (B) Fluorescence emission spectra of CNPs at different pH, insert: the color images of UV-lamp of CNP; (C) TEM image of CDs, inset: HRTEM image of CDs; (D) UV–vis spectra and fluorescence emission spectra of CDs, insert: the color of CDs under sunlight and UV-lamp.

(A) UV–vis absorption spectra of the different concentrations of GEN from 10−4 μg/mL to 1 μg/mL, insert: images of sunlight generated by systems with different concentrations of GEN; (B) Linear correlation curve and calibration curve of absorbance intensity ratio (I510 nm/I440 nm) towards different levels of GEN; (C) Fluorescence emission spectra of the different concentrations of GEN from 10−4 μg/mL to 1 μg/mL, insert: images of UV-lamp generated by systems with different concentrations of GEN; (D) Linear correlation curve and calibration curve of fluorescence emission intensity ratio (F550 nm/F450 nm) towards different levels of GEN.

(A) Linear correlation curve and calibration curve of R/G towards different levels of GEN (5 × 10−4, 2.5 × 10−2, 5 × 10−2, 1 × 10−1, 2 × 10−1 μg/mL) and the linear correlation curve; (B) Linear correlation curve and calibration curve of R/B towards different levels of GEN (5 × 10−4, 2.5 × 10−2, 5 × 10−2, 1 × 10−1, 2 × 10−1 μg/mL) and the linear correlation curve;(C) RGB analysis of colorimetric/fluorescent images via a color recognizer APP in the smartphone; (D) Standard color cards for the response of the sensing system to different concentrations of ofloxacin.
Mechanism diagram of on-site dual-mode immunosensor of GEN.

head.ce:title: Colorimetry/fluorescence dual-mode detection of gentamicin based on curcumin nanoparticles and manganese dioxide nanosheets

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Food Science and Technology, Bohai University, National & Local Joint Engineering Research Center of Storage, Processing and Safety Control Technology for Fresh Agricultural and Aquatic Products, Food Safety Key Lab of Liaoning Province, Institute of Ocean Research, The Fresh Food Storage and Processing Technology Research Institute of Liaoning Provincial Universities, Jinzhou, Liaoning 121013, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Food Science and Technology
National & Local Joint Engineering Research Center of Storage
Processing and Safety Control Technology for Fresh Agricultural and Aquatic Products
Food Safety Key Lab of Liaoning Province
The Fresh Food Storage and Processing Technology Research Institute of Liaoning Provincial Universities

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Food Science and Technology, Bohai University, National & Local Joint Engineering Research Center of Storage, Processing and Safety Control Technology for Fresh Agricultural and Aquatic Products, Food Safety Key Lab of Liaoning Province, Institute of Ocean Research, The Fresh Food Storage and Processing Technology Research Institute of Liaoning Provincial Universities, Jinzhou, Liaoning 121013, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This study used curcumin nanoparticles (CNPs) in phosphate buffer solution (pH 9) [CNPs (pH 9)] as a new oxidative substrate to detect gentamicin (GEN). Manganese dioxide nanosheets (MnO2-BSA NS) with excellent oxidizing properties were applied to catalytic oxidize CNPs (pH 9), which resulted in visual color changed from red to orange-yellow under sunlight. Moreover, the fluorescence of CNPs (pH 9) changed from orange to yellow. And we introduced carbon dots (CDs) as a reference signal, the fluorescence of CNPs quenched the fluorescence of CDs to generate a ratiometric fluorescence response. Therefore, we semi-qualitatively determined GEN with the naked eye and quantitatively evaluated by ratiometric colorimetry (I510 nm/I436 nm) and ratiometric fluorescence (F560 nm/F436 nm). The dual-mode immunoassay linearly correlated with GEN ranged from 10−4 μg/mL to 100 μg/mL with a detection limit (LOD) of 4.44 × 10−5 μg/mL and 3.16 × 10−5 μg/mL. Meanwhile, a smartphone-assisted system was designed for sensing of GEN by the distinct color change, providing a dependable, convenient and on-site assay of detecting GEN.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Gentamicin (GEN) is a type of aminoglycoside antibiotics, and its main mechanism is to quickly bind with ribosomes, effectively preventing bacterial protein synthesis and achieving antibacterial goals[1]. GEN finds frequent application in industries related to livestock, poultry, water and so on. Adding it to feed not only inhibits bacteria and prevents diseases, but also promotes animal growth. Although GEN has a high antibacterial effect, excessive and unreasonable use may lead to residual antibiotics in animal food, which can cause various toxic effects in humans after ingestion, including nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity [2]. At present, traditional methods for detecting GEN are difficult to meet the needs of on-site testing and screening due to their time-consuming, labor-intensive, high cost, and expensive equipment [3]. Accordingly, it is beneficial to create a simple, inexpensive, and sensitive method to detect residual GEN in animal derived foods.

Ratiometric fluorescence is a method of quantitative detection of targets using two different emission wavelengths [4]. It can not only greatly overcome external environmental interference and correct background interference, but also be used for sensitive quantitative analysis [5]. Colorimetric is a qualitative evaluation method that uses the naked eye to quickly read color changes, which profits by simplicity, low cost, quickly and strong intuitiveness [6]. Immunoassay mainly relies on highly specific antigen antibody binding reactions, and efficient biocatalytic reactions based on antigen antibody binding reactions can read out signals generated by the system for quantitative detection of analytes [7]. It has the advantages of high selectivity, flexible operation, high throughput and safety. Combined colorimetric and ratiometric fluorescence methods with immunoassay to construct a dual-mode immunosensor, compared with single-mode, dual-mode analysis has high sensitivity and strong selectivity, which can achieve qualitative and quantitative detection, avoid incorrect positive and negative outcomes, reduce errors and the possibility of missed detections, and ensure the sensitivity and accuracy of detection [8,9]. At the same time, both modes can also broaden the application scenarios of the method.

Curcumin (Cur), a widely found polyphenol, is derivable from turmeric [10], and its possible biological advantages, like antibacterial properties, have attracted considerable attention [11], anti-inflammatory [12], antioxidant [13] and so on. Nonetheless, Cur was hindered by issues like low solubility in water, volatile chemical characteristics, and swift photodegradation [14]. Addressing these issues, numerous research efforts have involved dissolving Cur in organic solvents or water-mixing organic solutions to create organic nanoaggregates through the reprecipitation technique, known as Cur nanoparticles (CNPs). CNPs in phosphate buffer solution (pH 9) [CNPs (pH 9)] showed red and the fluorescence of CNPs (pH 9) displayed orange. When CNPs (pH 9) were catalytic oxidized to generate intramolecular cyclization products containing tetrahydrofuran cyclopentanedione, resulting in a color transition from red to orange yellow and the fluorescence of CNPs (pH 9) changed from orange to yellow. The color changes of CNPs (pH 9) reduced the demand for additional luminescent material, thereby minimizing the interference of environmental factors [15]. The detection system based on the color change of CNPs (pH 9) exhibits an obvious visual visibility, possessing an outstanding development prospect in fluorescence/colorimetric-based applications of visual analysis. Nevertheless, detection platforms using CNPs (pH 9) as fluorescence/colorimetric-based catalytic oxidized substrate are rarely reported.

In this work, the dual-mode immunoassay was proposed based on the catalytic oxidation of CNPs (pH 9) by MnO2 nanosheet (MnO2-BSA NS). MnO2-BSA NS with large surface areas and strong oxidizing properties. The concept illustrated in Scheme 1 within this study, we used BSA-directed to synthesize MnO2-BSA NS by the wet-chemistry method. BSA couples with Ab2 by covalent interactions to form MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2. MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 were used to catalytic oxidize CNPs (pH 9), the absorbance of CNPs (pH 9) at 510 nm gradually decreased, while the absorbance at 440 nm gradually increased, and color reversibly with from red to orange yellow. And the fluorescence emission peak of CNPs (pH 9) shifted from 580 nm to 560 nm and gradually increased, the fluorescence changed from orange to yellow. Carbon dots (CDs) were introduced as a reference signal owing to the elevated rate of brightness and resistance to light. A critical factor was that inner filter effect (IFE) induced the quenching fluorescence of CDs by CNPs (pH 9) and establish a connection between ratiometric fluorescence. Based on the above, we adopt the dual-mode (ratiometric fluorescence/ratiometric colorimetric) immunoassay GEN. Firstly, Ab1 is fixed on a microporous plate, in the absence of GEN, there was a large amount of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 in the supernatant. The solution color was orange-yellow under sunlight and the fluorescence color was yellow under UV-lamp. When added GEN antigen, GEN antigen specifically binds to MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2. The more antigen-Ab1 and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 on the microplate, the less MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 in the supernatant. Therefore, the oxidation reaction of CNPs (pH 9) was reduced, the solution color was red under sunlight and the fluorescence color was blue under UV-lamp. In addition, using a smartphone app to recognize color changes and establish the relationship between R/G and R/B values and GEN concentration, achieving real-time on-site detection of GEN.
The material and instrument used in this work are described in the Supplementary material.

Preparation of CNPs (pH 9) used reprecipitation method. Shortly, 10 mg of Cur powder dissolved in 10 mL of ethanol absolute by ultrasound 10 min to obtain CNPs. Then, CNPs mixed phosphate buffer solution (pH 9) to gain CNPs (pH 9).

The synthesis method was slightly modified on the basis of a previous study [16]. 0.0346 g of MnAc2 were mixed into 10 ml of highly purified water and then combined with 10 ml of a 5 mg/mL BSA solution. Subsequently, the blended solution underwent incubation for one hour at ambient temperature and the pH condition was regulated to about 10 with NaOH solution. The mixture solution uninterruptedly shook on a shaker at ambient temperature for 10 h. Finally, the solution was centrifuged (10,000 rpm, 10 min) and rinsed three times with ultrapure water and preserved at a temperature of 4 °C for subsequent utilization.

3 mL of MnO2-BSA NS dissolved in PBS (0.01 M, pH 7.4) solution containing 1 mL of EDC (2 mg/mL) and 1 mL of NHS (2 mg/mL). After 30 min activation of the carboxylic groups, 1 mL of Ab2 (10 mg/mL) was mixed into solution with continuously stirring for 12 h. Ultimately, the blend underwent centrifugation (10,000 rpm, 10 min), was washed thrice with PBS (0.01 M, pH 7.4), and preserved at 4 °C for subsequent application

CDs synthesis techniques were derived from earlier scholarly works [17]. Shortly, a mixture of citric acid (5.26 g) and ethylenediamine (2.5 mL) was mixed into 50 mL of highly purified water. Subsequently, the blended solution was transferred into a container for pressing autoclaves. A peak temperature of 180 °C was maintained for a duration of 6 h. CD synthesis was chilled to ambient temperature, spun in a centrifuge, and passed through a 0.22 μM microporous membrane to eliminate impurities, then preserved at 4 °C.

Firstly, Ab1 (300 μL, 10 μg/mL) was dropped and left to incubate overnight at 4 °C within the 24-microwell plates. After incubation, each well of plates was rinsed three times with PBST (pH 7.4, 0.01 M). Subsequently, each well received an addition of BSA (300 μL, 1 %) to prevent nonspecific blocking, followed by a 1-hour incubation at 37 °C, PBST was used to cleanse the plates of any excess BSA. Subsequently, 300 μL of GEN antigens, varying in concentration, were introduced, incubated for one hour at 37 °C, and then cleansed thrice using PBST. Similarly, each well was treated with MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 (200 μL, 0.45 mg/mL) at a temperature of 37 °C for a duration of 1 h. A pipette was used to extract the mixed solution from each well and added to 1 mL CNPs (pH 9). After 8 min, measured the values by UV spectrophotometer (440 nm, 510 nm) and the ratio of I510 nm to I440 nm (I510 nm/I440 nm) was calculated. When measuring the fluorescence intensity, the same pipette gun was used to suck out the liquid in each hole in the 24-well microplate. The pH of the solution was adjusted to 9 with PBS of pH 11, and added to CNPs (pH9) and 200 μL CDs. After 10 min, the fluorescence intensity at 436 nm (F436 nm) and 560 nm (F560 nm) at the excitation wavelength of 360 nm was measured by fluorescence spectrophotometry, and the ratio of F560 nm to F436 nm (F560 nm/F436 nm) was calculated.

To evaluate the precision and interference-free nature of the suggested dual-mode immunoassay, five different antibacterial drugs, including two structural analogues of GEN (Kanamycin, Streptomycin) and three other antibacterial drugs (Norfloxacin, Sulfadiazine, Furazolidone) and seven interfering ions (Fe2+, Fe3+, K+, Ca2+, Mg2+, Na+, Zn2+), two amino acids (Tryptophan, Glycine) were selected. Every phase of the testing process for specificity and anti-interference was conducted following the procedures outlined in section 2.7.

The method for preparing reaction solutions followed the procedure outlined in section 2.6. Then the procedure involved capturing the pictures of the solution using sunlight and a UV-lamp emitting 365 nm of light. Collecting red (R), blue (B) and green (G) values of these pictures through a color recognition APP (Color Picker) of smartphone, and established the relationship between R/G, R/B values and GEN concentration to achieve on-site dual-mode immunoassay for GEN.

The processing of fish, salmon, shrimp, and chicken followed the procedure outlined in the previous literature [18], with minor alterations. 5 g of the specimens were placed into 50 mL centrifuge tubes. To extract the analytes, a mixture of 25 mL ethyl acetate was prepared, vortexed for 15 min, spun at 4000 rpm for 10 min, and then supernatant was gathered. Then repeated the step again. Subsequently, the gathered supernatant underwent rotary steaming to achieve a concentration of 5 mL. To eliminate fat, 5 mL of n-hexane was combined with the mixed mixture, vortexed for 30 s and spun at a speed of 3000 revolutions per minute for five minutes. The lower liquid was reserved for use. Diverse concentrations of GEN antigens (0.01, 0.1, 1 μg/mL) were incorporated into the acquired liquid, absorbance and fluorescence measurements were documented as outlined in sections 2.6. and 2.8.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterizations of MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2
ce:section.ce:para: TEM image of MnO2-BSA NS showed a large two-dimensional structure (Fig. 1 A), with occasional folds and folded representative 2D sheet nanostructures, indicating that the nanosheets have a large surface area. The average particle size of MnO2-BSA NS was 220 nm (Fig. 1B), which was accordant with the results of reference [16]. BSA had an obvious absorption peak centered at 278 nm, which might originate from the disulfide bonds and aromatic residues of BSA macromolecule. MnO2 had a well-defined exciton band at 323 nm [19,20] The color of MnO2-BSA NS was brown and a broad range absorbance peak of 200–700 nm was observed and there was a typical absorption peak at 310 nm (Fig. 1C), which was almost consistent with previous report [21]. The results proved successful synthesis of MnO2-BSA NS. Meanwhile, as FTIR analysis in Fig. 1D, a characteristic peak appeared around 500 cm−1 to stretching vibration of Mn-O corresponding to MnO2-BSA NS, which further indicted successful synthesis of MnO2-BSA NS [22]. The typical peaks of BSA at 1650 cm−1 (amide I, C=O stretching) and 1541 cm−1 (amide II, N–H bending) were shown in FT-IR spectra of all samples [23]. The results indicated that BSA had been successfully adhered on the surface of MnO2 NS.

Furthermore, XPS spectroscopy was employed to more thoroughly analyze the elements and chemical bonds within MnO2-BSA NS. The full spectrum XPS of MnO2-BSA NS had four characteristic peaks at 642, 530, 285, and 400 eV corresponding to Mn2p, O1s, C1s, and N1s (Fig. 2 A). For the XPS spectrum of Mn2p (Fig. 2B), the characteristic peaks at 653.6 eV and 641.3 eV represented Mn2p 1/2 and Mn2p 3/2 and testified that the manganese element was the only form of MnO2-BSA NS and Mn4+ was the main state of existence in nanomaterials [20]. In addition, the XPS spectrum of O1s (Fig. 2C) had a characteristic peak at 529.8 eV (Mn-O-Mn). While the XPS spectrum of C1s (Fig. 2D) had two characteristic peaks at 288.1 eV (C=O) and 284.8 eV (C–C/C–H), indicating the presence of BSA in MnO2-BSA NS, which were almost consistent with previous literature [16]. Meanwhile, we recorded the data of zeta potential to prove successful coupling between Ab2 and MnO2-BSA NS. In Fig. 3 E, the zeta potentials of MnO2-BSA NS, Ab2 and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 were −20.45 mV, −22.4 mV and −26.09 mV, the changes of zeta potentials verified Ab2 successful loded onto the surface of MnO2-BSA NS.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optical characterization of CNPs, CNPs (pH 9) and CDs
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Fig. 3A and B, CNPs exhibited a clear absorption peak at 430 nm and the fluorescence intensity at 550 nm. The solution of CNPs appeared yellow under sunlight and appeared yellow under UV lamp irradiation at 365 nm. When CNPs were added to PBS solution (pH 9), the peak of absorption observed at 430 nm red transitioned to 440 nm and a new absorption characteristic peak was generated at 510 nm, the solution color turned from yellow to red. Meanwhile, the fluorescence emission peak red shifted from 550 nm to 580 nm, and the fluorescence signal became from yellow to orange. The redshift of absorption spectra and fluorescence spectra may be due to changes in pH conditions, causing the charge within the molecule to transfer from the benzene ring to the carbonyl group, resulting in a change in the diketone structure of CNPs. Ultimately, the enhancement of electronegativity leads to a dark effect, resulting in a change in the maximum wavelength and intensity [24]. The generation of new absorption characteristic peaks may be give rise to the degradation of CNPs into ferulic acid and methane form [25]. As revealed in Fig. 3C, the TEM scan of the CDs revealed a consistent quasi-spherical form, featuring a lattice gap of 0.21 nm which be associated with the [110] direction of graphite [26]. In Fig. 3D, CDs exhibited an absorbance level of 342 nm. Moreover, CDs had a blue fluorescence at 450 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The mechanism of dual-mode immunoassay for GEN
ce:section.ce:para:
At alkaline pH, curcumin can undergo intramolecular cyclization reaction with phenoxy radical intermediates generated by autooxidation or reactive oxygen species (ROS)-mediated, which changes the long-chain conjugated β-enedione segments between two benzene rings to produce intramolecular cyclization products with tetrahydrofuran-fused cyclopentadione structure [27]. In Fig. 2 B, the peaks at 653.6 and 641.3 eV corresponded to Mn 2p1/2 and Mn 2p3/2, respectively. Moreover, MnO2 could be further deconvoluted into Mn4+, Mn3+ and Mn2+ oxidation states. Mn3+ and Mn2+ would generate oxygen vacancies to activate O2, thus forming active species superoxide anions (O2
−) [28]. Radicals subject organic dye molecules (Cur) to a series of oxidation and reduction reactions [19].

After oxidation with MnO2-BSA NS, the color of CNPs (pH 9) varied from red to orange- yellow under sunlight, and the absorbance at 510 nm decreased, while the absorbance at 440 nm increased. This is attributed to the two UV absorption characteristic peaks of CNPs (pH 9) before oxidation, which are the absorption of the benzene ring and the long-chain conjugated K-band, respectively. After oxidation, CNPs (pH 9) generated intramolecular cyclization products with tetrahydrofuran cyclopentanedione, leading to the destruction of the long-chain conjugated structure in the chemical structure. The configuration of the oxidation products changes, and the corresponding K-band absorption weakens, resulting in different absorption spectra of catalytic oxidized CNPs (pH 9) compared to those before oxidation. Study the mechanism of CNPs (pH 9) on fluorescence quenching of CDs (Fig. S1A and B), the absorption spectrum of CNPs (pH 9) overlapped significantly with the emission spectra and excitation spectra of CDs, illustrating that fluorescent resonance energy transfer (FRET) or IFE led to a fluorescence quenching effect of CDs. Our study focused on examining the fluorescence lifetime of CDs before and after adding CNPs (p H 9). From Fig. S1C, it can be seen that there was no sharp distinction between the two in the fluorescence lifetime. Therefore, the fluorescence of CDs was quenched by CNPs (pH 9) excluded FRET and related to IFE.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Oxidase activity study of MnO2-BSA NS
ce:section.ce:para: To verify the catalytic oxidase activity of CNPs (pH 9) by MnO2-BSA NS, UV–vis absorption spectrum and fluorescence spectrum were recorded. Meanwhile, TMB as a typical oxidase chromogenic substrate, the catalytic oxidation activity of MnO2-BSA NS on TMB was studied and its UV–vis absorption spectra was recorded. The UV–vis absorption spectrum of MnO2-BSA NS catalytic oxidized CNPs (pH 9) showed that with MnO2-BSA NS increased, the absorbance at 510 nm tended upward and changed increase at 440 nm and the color of solution changed from red to orange yellow under sunlight (Fig. S2A). The fluorescence spectrum of MnO2-BSA NS catalytic oxidized CNPs (pH 9) showed that as MnO2-BSA NS increased, the fluorescence emission peak of CNPs (pH 9) blue shifted from 580 nm to 560 nm and gradually increased, the color of solution varied from orange to yellow under UV-lamp (Fig. S2B). The UV absorption spectrum of MnO2-BSA NS oxidized TMB showed that as MnO2-BSA NS increased, the absorbance at 650 nm of oxTMB showed an enhance trend and the solution's hue shifted from being colorless to blue under sunlight (Fig. S2C). The above results provided a provment for excellent oxidase activity of MnO2-BSA NS.

To further investigate the catalytic oxidized activity of MnO2-BSA NS, steady-state kinetic experiments were conducted. When the TMB concentration was in the range of 2–7 mM, Vmax and Km kinetic data were obtained through Lineweaver Burk plot and Mie equation. The smaller Km proves that the stronger affinity is exhibited between the enzyme and substrate. The Km of MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 were 0.167 mM and 0.104 mM, respectively (Table S1). Compared with nanoenzymes in the literature [29,30], their Km was smaller, indicating that MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 have stronger affinity for substrates and stronger catalytic activity. The Vmax of MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 are 6.1 × 10−8 M/s and 7.2 × 10−8 M/s, respectively. Compared with nanoenzymes in the literature [29,30], their Vmax was larger, indicating that MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 have higher catalytic activity. Comparing the Km and Vmax of MnO2-BSA NS and MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2, the Km of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 was smaller than that of MnO2-BSA NS, and the Vmax of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 is greater than that of MnO2-BSA NS, indicating that MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 had stronger affinity and higher catalytic activity. The modification of Ab2 improved the stability of MnO2-BSA NS, thereby improving the enzyme-like activity of MnO2-BSA NS [31].

Attaining conditions for optical detection and enhanced sensitivity, the concentration of PBS (pH 9), the concentration of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 and reaction times were optimized. The concentration of PBS (pH 9) can promote the sensitive change of the color of the entire system. The concentration of PBS (pH 9) was 1 mM, the values of I510 nm/I440 nm reached maximum and the values of F560 nm/F436 nm reached minimum. After exceeding 1 mM, the values of I510 nm/I440 nm and F560 nm/F436 nm tended to equilibrium (Fig. S3A), indicating that 1 mM PBS (pH 9) was sufficient to change the color of CNPs and presented the optimum signal response in the detection system. When the ionic strength increases, it will affect the aggregation of nanozymes, thus affecting the surface stability of nanozymes [32]. Therefore, 1 mM of PBS (pH 9) was used for the detection. As shown in Fig. S3B, when MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 was 0.45 mg/mL, the values of I510 nm/I440 nm reached maximum and the values of F560 nm/F436 nm reached minimum. Moreover, the values of I510 nm/I440 nm and F560 nm/F436 nm tended to equilibrium after 0.45 mg/mL of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2, because 0.45 mg/mL MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 was sufficient to catalytic oxidize the certain concentration of substrate. Therefore, 0.45 mg/mL of MnO2-BSA NS-Ab2 was selected. The reaction times largely affected whether MnO2-BSA NS had good catalytic activity towards CNPs (pH 9). As shown in Fig. S3C, the value of I510 nm/I440 nm reached minimum value at 8 min and tended to equilibrium after 8 min, while the F560 nm/F436 nm value reached maximum at 10 min and tended to equilibrium after 10 min. The results illustrated that MnO2-BSA NS was sufficient to catalytic oxidize CNPs (pH 9) and achieve maximum color rendering at the optimal times. Therefore, the optimal times were selected 8 min (ratiometric colorimetry) and 10 min (ratiometric fluorescence).

In ideal circumstances, our research focused on the dual-mode immunoassay single, utilizing ratiometric colorimetry and fluorescence techniques with differing GEN concentrations (0.0001 μg/mL to 1 μg/mL). As shown in Fig. 4 A, for the ratiometric colorimetry method, as the levels of GEN antigens rose, the absorption peak at 440 nm increased, while at 510 nm, it decreased. Moreover, the solution's hue shifts from red to orange yellow. Fig. 4B displayed an effective linear equation correlating the logarithmic magnitude of GEN and I510 nm/I440 nm, the calibration graph showed Y = 0.771 + 0.077logX (R2 = 0.9951), with the limit of detection (LOD) of 4.44 × 10−5 μg/mL. Similarly, for the ratiometric fluorescence method (Fig. 4C), with the increase of GEN antigens, the fluorescence of CNPs (pH 9) at 560 nm raised, the fluorescence of CDs at 440 nm reduced. Meanwhile, the fluorescence of solution changed from yellow to blue under UV lamp. Fig. 4D displayed an effective linear equation linking the logarithmic value of GEN and F560 nm/F440 nm, the calibration graph was Y = 1.153–0.568logX (R2 = 0.9974), LOD of 3.16 × 10−5 μg/mL.

The precision of the immunoassay technique was primarily influenced by the selected antibody. The Ab1 used in this study demonstrated strong specificity towards GEN antigens. For ratiometric colorimetry method, as seen in Fig. 4A, the measurements of I510 nm/I440 nm generated by these antibiotics matched those of the blank signals, significantly lower than GEN. For ratiometric fluorescence method, as seen in Fig. 4C, the values of F560 nm/F436 nm generated by these antibiotics matched those of the blank signals, significantly exceeding GEN. In Fig. S4B and D, it's noticeable that the I510 nm/I440 nm and F560 nm/F436 nm values generated by these interfering substances matched those from GEN, signifying that these substances did not influence GEN detection. In conclusion, the findings revealed that the dual-mode immunoassay emphasizes its high specificity and resistance to interference in detecting GEN.

To evaluate the excellent sensitivity and practicality of dual-mode immunoassay, we used the proposed method and GEN commercial reagent kit to detect GEN in different actual samples. No GEN was detected in all samples before labeling. Therefore, different concentrations of GEN including 1 × 10−2, 10 × 10−2 and 100 × 10−2 μg/mL were spiked into samples (fish, chicken, shrimp and pig) to carry out recovery experiment and calculate recovery. As Table S2 shown, recoveries of 92.2–101.3 % and a RSD of 3.3–5.5 % for the spiked samples were achieved using the ratiometric colorimetry method. Recoveries of 90.5–102.3 % and a RSD of 3.1–5.1 % for the spiked samples were achieved for the ratiometric fluorescence method. The results indicated the favorable accuracy and precision of dual-mode immunoassay. Moreover, the outcomes achieved through a dual-mode immunoassay matched those obtained using the GEN commercial ELISA kit, the findings further confirmed the feasibility and accuracy of the newly created dual-mode immunoassay in determining actual samples.

The method of mobile on-site detection, aided by smartphones, offers affordability, minimal workload, and convenient usability. As shown Fig. 5 C, to detect GEN, a platform aided by smartphones was created, which showed clear alterations in the solution's color when exposed to sunlight and a UV lamp. Fig. 5D is a standard color card, which can be used as a reference for on-site rapid semi-quantitative detection. By capturing photos of the solution, our research focused on the R/G and R/B ratios using smartphones, examining GEN concentrations ranging from 5 × 10−4 μg/mL to 2 × 10−1 μg/mL. As shown Fig. 5A, the linear relation equation between R/G and the concentrations of GEN was Y = 1.58 + 7.289X (R2 = 0.9941) and featuring a detection limit of 3.12 × 10−4 μg/mL. As shown Fig. 5B, the linear relation equation between R/B and the concentrations of GEN was Y = 4.545–1.393X (R2 = 0.9892) and featuring a detection limit of 2.91 × 10−4 μg/mL.

Additionally, for assessment purposes the preciseness and practicality of the proposed smartphone based dual-mode immunoassay method for detecting GEN in actual samples, the shrimp samples were tested using the proposed smartphone based dual-mode immunoassay method. The results showed that GEN was not detected in the shrimp samples before spiking. So, different concentrations of GEN (0.025 μ g/mL, 0.1 μ g/mL, and 0.2 μ g/mL) were added to shrimp samples for spiked recovery experiments in Table S3. As Table S3 shown, recoveries of 91.0–99.6 % and a RSD of 2.3–5.0 % for the spiked samples were achieved using the ratiometric colorimetry method. Recoveries of 92.8–100.5 % and a RSD of 2.9–4.3 % for the spiked samples were achieved for the ratiometric fluorescence method. The satisfactory findings revealed that the suggested smartphone based dual-mode immunoassay accurate detected GEN. It can realize lost cost on-site testing GEN. In addition, the dual-mode immunoassay of reproducibility and intra/inter-batch variations were also calculated, as shown in Table S4. RSD of 2.61–5.08 % for the Intra-assay and 3.77–4.50 % for the Inter-assay. Therefore, the sensor has good stability and reusability.

In summary, we synthesized MnO2-BSA NS with rich surface functional groups guided by BSA, and used CNPs (pH 9) as the catalytic substrate. After catalytic oxidation by MnO2-BSA NS, the color transition of the CNPs (pH 9) from red to orange yellow to achieve ratiometric colorimetric detection. At the same time, introducing CDs as reference signal for ratiometric fluorescence, CNPs (pH 9) had a quenching effect on CDs and the fluorescence color became from yellow to blue to achieve ratiometric fluorescence detection. Finally, sensitive dual-mode immune detection of GEN was achieved through sandwich immunoassay. Under the optimal experimental conditions, with a detection range of 10−4–1 μg/mL, with LODs of 4.44 × 10−5 μg/mL (ratiometric colorimetric) and 3.16 × 10−5 μg/mL (ratiometric fluorescence). In addition, the proposed method had good recoveries in animal derived food and was consistent with the detection results of the GEN commercial reagent kit, proving the practicality and accuracy of this method. At the same time, the detection of GEN was achieved the sensor, with a detection range of 5 × 10−4 μg/mL–2 × 10−1 μg/mL and LODs of 3.12 × 10−4 μg/mL (colorimetric) and 2.91 × 10−4 μg/mL (fluorescence). Using MnO2-BSA NS as the catalyst and CNPs (pH 9) as the catalytic substrate, significant changes in color were observed under sunlight and UV-lamp, enhancing the output of color signals. Not only can it perform semi-quantitative detection with the naked eye, but it can also introduce smartphone sensors for real-time on-site quantitative detection. The developed dual-mode immunosensor enhanced application potential in the field of food detection.
This research did not receive any specific grant from funding agencies in the public, commercial, or not-for-profit sectors.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2025.126419.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
